person.name: Eleanor FitzHugh

memoryArtifacts.headline: Tunstal of Thurland Castle
memoryArtifacts.description: This chart comes from the book  The Redmans of Levens and Harewood.  Pg 220

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Aleonora "Eleanor" FitzHugh (b. c. 1391 Ravensworth, co. Richmond - d. 30 Sep 1457 at Newington, co. Middlesex).

«b»Marriage«/b»
Elizabeth married three times. Roskell (1981), states that:

"The mother of the Darcy heiresses, Eleanor FitzHugh, after the death of their father, married as her second husband Thomas Tunstall of Thurland (in Londsdale, Lancs.).... The most important of her children by this alliance was her son, Sir Richard Tunstall of Bentham (Yorks.) and Thurland (Lancs.), a Lancastrian courtier who was esquire of the body to Henry VI from 1452 to 1455, king's carver from 1450 to 1460, chamberlain of Chester from 1457 to 1460, and chamberlain of the exchequer from late 1459 until Edward IV's accession in 1461; he fought as a Lancastrian at Wakefield and Towton, escaped to Scotland, was attainted in 1461, went off to join the small band of Lancastrian devotees after the fall of Dunstanborough, and was still with Henry VI at the time of his capture in 1465; not until 1468 did he surrender at Harlech in return for a pardon. Eleanor FitzHugh by March 1453 had married, as her third husband, Henry Bromflete, Lord Vessy, who was summoned to Parliament as a baron between 1449 and his death in 1469; her daughter by this marriage, Strangeways' wife's young half-sister, married the Lancastrian Lord ("Butcher") Clifford.

«b»Marriage«/b»
m.1 (11 Oct 1411 settlement) Sir Philip Darcy, 6th Baron Darcy, Lord Meinell (d. 02 Aug 1418), son of Sir John Darcy, 5th Baron Darcy, 4th Lord Meinell and Margaret Grey. The marriage took place before 28 October 1412. The couple had 2 daughters:

1.) Elizabeth m. Sir James Strangeways

2.) Margery m. Sir John Conyers

m.2 (ante 18 Feb 1427) Sir Thomas Tunstall, son of Sir Thomas Tunstall, Justice of the Peace for Westmorland and Isabel Harrington. Issue: 3 sons, 2 dau:

1.) Sir Richard

2.) Thomas, esq

3.) William, esq.

4.) Elizabeth/Isabel m. Sir Simon Norwich

5.) Joan m. Sir Roger Warde; m.2 Sir William Stapleton

m.3 (after 12 Apr 1434) Sir Henry Bromflete de Vescy (d. 16 Jan 1468/9; bur. Whitefriars, London), Issue: 1 daughter:

1.) Margaret, m.1 Sir John, 9th Lord Clifford, m.2 Sir Lancelot Threlkeld

zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor FitzHugh於大約1395年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾拉文斯沃斯時，她的父親Sir Henry FitzHugh39歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Grey33歲。她於大約1412年與Sir Philip Darcy結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1457年9月30日在英國倫敦享年63歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor FitzHugh于大约1395年出生在英国英格兰越可爾拉文斯沃斯时，她的父亲Sir Henry FitzHugh39岁，母亲Lady Elizabeth Grey33岁。她于大约1412年与Sir Philip Darcy结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世于1457年9月30日在英国倫敦享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: FitzHugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor FitzHugh was born about 1395, in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Henry FitzHugh, was 39 and her mother, Lady Elizabeth Grey, was 33. She married Sir Philip Darcy about 1412. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died on 30 September 1457, in London, England, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor FitzHugh est née environ 1395, à Ravensworth, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Henry FitzHugh, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Grey, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Philip Darcy environ 1412. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 30 septembre 1457, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor FitzHugh um 1395 in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Henry FitzHugh 39 und ihre Mutter Lady Elizabeth Grey 33. Sie hat um 1412 Sir Philip Darcy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 30. September 1457 im Alter von 63 Jahren in London, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor FitzHugh è nata il circa 1395 nel Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Henry FitzHugh, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Lady Elizabeth Grey, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Sir Philip Darcy circa 1412. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 30 settembre 1457, nel London, England, all'età di 63 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor FitzHughが約1395年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアラベンスウオルスで生まれた時，父Sir Henry FitzHughは39歳，母Lady Elizabeth Greyは33歳であった。約1412年にSir Philip Darcyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1457年9月30日にイギリスロンドンで63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor FitzHugh(이)가 대략 1395년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 레이븐스워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Henry FitzHugh(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Grey(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 대략 1412년 Sir Philip Darcy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1457년 9월 30일 영국 런던에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor FitzHugh nasceu aproximadamente 1395, em Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Henry FitzHugh, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Lady Elizabeth Grey, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Philip Darcy aproximadamente 1412. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 30 de setembro de 1457, em London, Inglaterra, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor FitzHugh родилась около 1395 г. в местности равенсворт, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Henry FitzHugh было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Grey, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Philip Darcy около 1412 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла 30 сентября 1457 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 63 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eleanor FitzHugh nació aproximadamente 1395, en Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Henry FitzHugh, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Lady Elizabeth Grey, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Sir Philip Darcy aproximadamente 1412. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 30 de septiembre de 1457, en London, Inglaterra, a la edad de 63 años.
